headline: How SK Hynix's bet on a niche memory chip made it more valuable than Samsung
summary: SK Hynix's overtaking of Samsung Electronics to become South Korea's most valuable firm was the culmination of 14 years of bets that brought it skepticism and scorn but ultimately put it at the centre of the global AI gold rush.  In 2012, conglomerate SK Group acquired Hynix Semiconductor in a deal ‌that was considered financially irresponsible.  Samsung, in contrast, was valued at more than 10 times SK Hynix and was the global leader in Dynamic Random-Access Memory (DRAM), a memory type that powers ‌laptops and smartphones.

content.title: How SK Hynix's bet on a niche memory chip made it more valuable than Samsung
content.summary: SK Hynix's overtaking of Samsung Electronics to become South Korea's most valuable firm was the culmination of 14 years of bets that brought it skepticism and scorn but ultimately put it at the centre of the global AI gold rush.  In 2012, conglomerate SK Group acquired Hynix Semiconductor in a deal ‌that was considered financially irresponsible.  Samsung, in contrast, was valued at more than 10 times SK Hynix and was the global leader in Dynamic Random-Access Memory (DRAM), a memory type that powers ‌laptops and smartphones.

headline: Qualcomm in talks to provide custom chip-design services to ByteDance, sources say
summary: Qualcomm is in talks to provide chip-design services to China's ByteDance, four people familiar with the matter said, as the U.S. company seeks ‌to reduce dependence on the smartphone market, its biggest revenue source.  If successful, the ‌negotiations would make ByteDance, the parent of short-video platform TikTok, an early customer of Qualcomm's chip-design services operation.  ​Qualcomm is the world's largest supplier of smartphone modem chips, which manage cellular communications.

content.title: Qualcomm in talks to provide custom chip-design services to ByteDance, sources say

headline: Cerebras sinks 14% as full-year margin forecast disappoints
summary: Cerebras shares tumbled about 14% before the bell on Wednesday after the chip designer warned that annual profit margins ‌would undershoot first-quarter figures in its debut earnings following a ‌blockbuster initial public offering.  Cerebras forecast adjusted gross margins of 38% to 41% for 2026, compared with the 47% it reported ‌for the first quarter.  The ⁠projection is far below those of rivals such as Nvidia's mid-70% range and Advanced Micro Devices' mid-50%, even as it ⁠came above analysts' estimates of 29.58%.

headline: Masayoshi Son Says Arm Holdings Could Be Worth $4 Trillion. Should You Believe Him?
summary: Artificial intelligence is reshaping the semiconductor industry in ways few investors anticipated just a few years ago. The early winners were obvious: Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) dominated AI accelerators, while memory makers like Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) are benefiting from soaring demand for high-bandwidth memory. Now the battle is shifting toward a less glamorous but equally important component ... Masayoshi Son Says Arm Holdings Could Be Worth $4 Trillion. Should You Believe Him?

headline: Time to Dump Micron? SK hynix to Begin Trading on Nasdaq July 10
summary: The artificial intelligence boom has created winners across the semiconductor industry, but few areas have benefited more recently than memory chips.
